goal: compose[Nat, M, N](f, p, n) = f(p(n))

goal: is_sequence_lift[M, N](f, p, q) = (compose[Nat, M, N](f, p) = q)
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0, x1: Nat -> T1, x2: Nat -> T0) { compose[Nat, T1, T0](x0, x1) = x2 = is_sequence_lift[T1, T0](x0, x1, x2) }[N, M](f, p, q)

function(x0: Nat) { f(p(x0)) = q(x0) }(w0)

goal: is_right_inverse_fn[M, N](f, g) = forall(x0: N) { f(g(x0)) = x0 }
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T1 -> T0, x1: T0 -> T1) { forall(x2: T0) { x0(x1(x2)) = x2 } = is_right_inverse_fn[T1, T0](x0, x1) }[N, M](f, g)

proof: function(x0: N -> M) { not is_right_inverse_fn[M, N](f, x0) }(g)

goal: is_isometry[N, M](g) = forall(x0: N, x1: N) { g(x0).distance(g(x1)) = x0.distance(x1) }
proof: function[T0: MetricSpace, T1: MetricSpace](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x1.distance(x2) = x0(x1).distance(x0(x2)) } = is_isometry[T0, T1](x0) }[N, M](g)
